title: Date, Time, and Time Zones for Web Services
breadcrumbs: SOAP Call-In to Uniface From a Web Service Client
Date, Time, and Time Zones for Web Services
text: Date, Time, and Time Zones for Web Services Web services are often used worldwide, where the client can be on one side of the world and the server on the other side. This has an impact on the handling of time and date information passed to and from the web service. The local time at the client side might differ by a number of hours from the local time at the server side. The XML schema data types date , time , and dateTime , passed in the SOAP request, may optionally include a time zone. Uniface date and time data types are local times but at a specific location—the time zone defined on the users computer. Conversions from Uniface to an XML schema always result in a UTC time-zoned date and time. Because Uniface has an implicit time zone, you cannot specify another time zone using a Uniface date or time. If you need to supply the time in another time zone as a parameter, you must calculate the time in your time zone or define the parameter as a string and provide a UTC date/time directly. Conversions from XML to Uniface should only use the time zone, if present, to convert to a local time at the user's location. If no time zone is included, the local date and time is considered to be in an unspecified location, so a time zone (or daylight savings time) can never be applied. If a time zone is included, the time zone supplies the offset in hours relative to GMT (Greenwich Mean Time), making the time information absolute to GMT. Daylight Savings Time The following restrictions are known concerning the determination of daylight saving time: Microsoft Windows cannot determine daylight saving time for a date before 1970 or after 2038, so Uniface does not perform time conversion Unix cannot determine daylight saving time for a date before 1901 or after 2038, so Uniface does not perform time conversion The following table shows examples of date and time conversion done by the Uniface Server, where the Uniface Server resides in GMT -6:00. Table 1. Date and time conversion done by the Uniface Server Date and/or time in SOAP request Date and/or time in UServer at GMT -6:00 No daylight saving time Daylight saving time of 1 hour 20:00:25+1:00 13:00:25 14:00:25 2002-01-24T20:00:25+1:00 1 2002-06-24 14:00:25 NA 2002-06-24T20:00:25+1:00 1 NA 2002-06-24 14:00:25 2002-01-24T01:00:25+1:00 2 2002-01-23 19:00:25 NA 20:00:25 3 20:00:25 21:00:25 20:00:25+0:00 4 14:00:25 15:00:25 20:00:25Z 4 14:00:25 15:00:25 The date in the SOAP request determines whether conversion is done with or without daylight saving time. The difference in time zones between client and server forces the date to go one day back. No time zone is supplied in the SOAP request, so no conversion. Time zone 0:00 is not the same as no time zone. Related Links Data Type Mapping for Web Service Call-In Uniface data types differ from the data types defined in the XML Schema Definition (XSD). To support Uniface Web service call-in, the Uniface data types must be mapped to the XML schema data types. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
